# Evaluation Scorecards

This directory contains evaluation scorecards generated by the skill-improvement tool.

## Structure

```
scorecards/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── llm-council/
│   └── scorecard.md          # Evaluation result for llm-council skill
├── using-superpowers/
│   └── scorecard.md          # Evaluation result for using-superpowers skill
└── <project-name>/
    └── scorecard.md          # Evaluation result for any project
```

## How Scorecards Are Generated

### Auto-Generated (Recommended)

When you run evaluation without specifying a scorecard path:

```bash
npx tsx packages/cli/src/bin.ts evaluate <project-path> <rubric-path>
```

The tool automatically creates:
- `scorecards/<project-name>/scorecard.md`
- `scorecards/<project-name>/history/<timestamp>.json`

### Custom Output Path

Specify a custom path:

```bash
npx tsx packages/cli/src/bin.ts evaluate \
  <project-path> \
  <rubric-path> \
  custom-path/my-scorecard.md
```

### With Output Directory

Specify an output directory:

```bash
npx tsx packages/cli/src/bin.ts evaluate \
  <project-path> \
  <rubric-path> \
  scorecards/<project-name>/
```

## Viewing Scorecards

### Markdown Scorecard

Open the `.md` file in any markdown viewer:

```bash
cat scorecards/llm-council/scorecard.md
```

### JSON History

View evaluation history for trend analysis:

```bash
cat scorecards/llm-council/history/2026-04-21T14-04-00-000Z.json
```

## Adding New Scorecards

When evaluating a new project, the tool will automatically create a new directory:

```bash
npx tsx packages/cli/src/bin.ts evaluate \
  /path/to/my/project \
  benchmark-profiles/enterprise-hello-world/rubric.json
```

This creates:
```
scorecards/my-project/
├── scorecard.md
└── history/
    ├── 2026-04-21T14-04-00-000Z.json
    └── 2026-04-21T15-00-00-000Z.json
```

## CI/CD Integration

In CI/CD, you can commit scorecards to track quality over time:

```yaml
- name: Evaluate and Save Scorecard
  run: |
    npx tsx packages/cli/src/bin.ts evaluate \
      . \
      benchmark-profiles/enterprise-hello-world/rubric.json \
      scorecards/$(basename $PWD)/scorecard.md

- name: Commit Scorecard
  run: |
    git add scorecards/
    git commit -m "Update scorecard for $(basename $PWD)"
```

## Best Practices

1. **Commit scorecards** to track quality over time
2. **Don't commit history** unless you need trend analysis (they can get large)
3. **Add `.gitignore`** for history if you don't need it:
   ```
   scorecards/*/history/
   ```
4. **Use descriptive rubric names** for easier identification
5. **Review scorecards** before merging to ensure quality gates are met
